aliases: 菲律宾刨冰甜品 (Fēilǜbīn Bào Bīng Tiánpǐn)

chinese: 菲律宾刨冰甜品 (Fēilǜbīn Bào Bīng Tiánpǐn)

food_subgroup: Shaved ice dessert with sweetened toppings (red beans, chickpeas, macapuno, jackfruit, kaong, leche flan, ube ice cream, evaporated milk)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Halo-Halo specifically. Closest parent would be a shaved ice dessert category; no exact FoodOn term available as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GBQ used as closest parent for shaved ice dessert category; no Halo-Halo-specific FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for Halo-Halo or Filipino shaved ice dessert category.

main_ingredients: ube ice cream (purple yam ice cream)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one tall glass of approximately 450g, comprising shaved ice layered with sweetened toppings, evaporated milk, leche flan, and ube ice cream, providing approximately 440 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Halo-Halo — FNRI Philippines PFCT component estimates cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredient components
nutrient_source.organization: FNRI-DOST Philippines (Philippine Food Composition Table) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Halo-Halo as a composite dish. Values are estimated from FNRI-DOST Philippines PFCT component data for individual toppings (sweetened red beans, jackfruit, coconut, ube) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for evaporated milk, leche flan analogue (egg custard), and ice cream components. Per-100g values derived from a modelled 450g glass composition: ~200g shaved ice, ~60ml evaporated milk, ~80g leche flan, ~60g ube ice cream, ~50g sweetened beans/legumes, ~50g mixed fruit toppings. Composition varies by vendor; values represent a mid-range estimate. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 as detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in FNRI PFCT or USDA SR Legacy at the composite level.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from dominant carbohydrate components: sucrose-sweetened legume toppings (GI ~65–70), sweetened fruit syrups, and ice cream analogues; reference anchored to University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened red beans (GI ~40), ice cream (GI ~62), and sucrose (GI ~65), weighted by carbohydrate contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Halo-Halo exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~70 is estimated from the weighted average of dominant carbohydrate-contributing components: sweetened red beans (GI ~40, University of Sydney GI Database), sucrose/sugar syrups (GI ~65), ube ice cream analogue (GI ~62 per ice cream reference, University of Sydney GI Database), and leche flan (GI ~65 estimated from egg custard with sucrose). The shaved ice format (rapid dissolution of sugars into liquid as ice melts) is expected to accelerate absorption, supporting a composite GI estimate at the higher end (~70). GL per serving = (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (70 x 72) / 100 = 50.4, where 72g carbohydrate is derived from 450g x 16.0g/100g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~180mg per 450g serving); primary cardiovascular concern is saturated fat from leche flan and ube ice cream, not sodium
hypertension.guidance: Halo-Halo poses minimal acute hypertension risk due to very low sodium (~180mg per glass), but the saturated fat content (~7g per serving from leche flan and ice cream) represents a long-term cardiovascular risk factor that hypertensive patients should monitor as part of overall dietary fat management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high glycemic load (GL ~50.4 per serving) driven by ~57g sugar and ~72g total carbohydrate from multiple rapidly-absorbed sweetened toppings; GI ~70 (high)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Halo-Halo is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to an extremely high glycemic load of ~50.4 per glass — equivalent to the highest-GL hawker noodle dishes — with ~57g of rapidly absorbed sugars that will cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed at all, portion should be limited to one-quarter of a glass shared among multiple people, and only on rare occasions with compensatory low-GI meals throughout the day.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~7.2g per 450g serving from leche flan and ube ice cream) combined with high sugar load (~57g) contributing to dyslipidaemia and insulin resistance risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Halo-Halo due to the combination of ~7g saturated fat per serving (primarily from leche flan egg custard and ube ice cream) and ~57g sugar, which together promote dyslipidaemia, elevated triglycerides, and insulin resistance — all established cardiovascular risk factors; occasional consumption (once monthly or less) with portion control is the maximum advisable frequency.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: 57g sugar per serving drives acute hyperglycaemia and pro-inflammatory cytokine release (IL-6, TNF-alpha, CRP); saturated fat adds further pro-inflammatory dietary load
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Halo-Halo as the 57g sugar load per glass represents the most pro-inflammatory single-food exposure in a typical diet, driving acute hyperglycaemia and systemic inflammatory cytokine release; if cultural context makes avoidance difficult, limit to twice yearly, share one glass among 3–4 people, and ensure all other meals that day are anti-inflammatory.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Halo-Halo (Tagalog: 'mix-mix') is the Philippines' signature dessert — a tall glass of shaved ice layered with an array of sweetened toppings and crowned with leche flan (egg custard caramel) and ube (purple yam) ice cream. The name reflects the instruction to mix everything together before eating. A standard serving is approximately 450g and contains: shaved ice as the base, evaporated milk poured over the ice, sweetened red beans (monggo), sweetened chickpeas (garbanzos), macapuno (coconut sport), sweetened jackfruit strips, kaong (sugar palm fruit), leche flan (~80g, ~200 kcal), and ube ice cream (~60g, ~130 kcal). Per glass (~450g): approximately 440 kcal, 8g protein, 12g fat (7g saturated), 72g carbohydrate, 57g sugar, ~180mg sodium. The glycemic index is approximately 70 (high) and the glycemic load per serving is approximately 50.4 — equivalent to the highest-GL hawker noodle dishes in Singapore, making this one of the most glycaemically impactful single-dessert items available. The shaved ice format may accelerate sugar absorption as ice melts and sugars dissolve into liquid form. Composition varies enormously by vendor (8–20 toppings possible). Available in Singapore at Jollibee and Filipino restaurants. Clinically, Halo-Halo is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing weight, and should be consumed only occasionally by the general population due to its extreme sugar density.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Halo-Halo as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from FNRI-DOST Philippines PFCT component data and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for individual ingredient components, modelled against a representative 450g glass composition. Composition varies significantly by vendor (number and quantity of toppings). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component references. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data from approved sources is robust and the modelled composition is representative of a standard commercial serving.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite shaved ice dessert is not available as a single entry in FNRI-DOST Philippines PFCT or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; component-level fatty acid data for leche flan and ube ice cream analogues was not available at sufficient granularity to produce a reliable composite estimate. water_g set to -1: water content for this composite dish (which includes shaved ice as a major component) is not reported as a single value in any approved source; the shaved ice base is predominantly water but the composite water_g for the full dish including all toppings is not available from FNRI PFCT or USDA SR Legacy. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for all component ingredients based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for evaporated milk, egg custard, and plant-based toppings. The health_suitability field from the input JSON records 'suitable: true' for diabetes — this has been corrected in health_context to 'concern_level: high' as a GL of 50.4 and 57g sugar per serving is clinically contraindicated for diabetes management. The input diabetes suitability flag appears to be a data entry error. Nutrient values represent a mid-range estimate for a standard commercial preparation; actual values may vary by ±20–30% depending on vendor topping quantities and sweetness levels.
